title: Fertilizer Dosage Calculator
description: Industrial-grade calculator for optimal fertilizer application rates based on crop needs, soil analysis, and application efficiency, following ISO 14051, Lean Six Sigma, and WERC guidelines.

inputs.label: Crop Type
inputs.businessContext: Determines the nitrogen (N), phosphorus (P), and potassium (K) uptake coefficients from agronomic tables.

businessContext_i18n.en: Determines the nitrogen (N), phosphorus (P), and potassium (K) uptake coefficients from agronomic tables.

inputs.label: Target Yield
inputs.businessContext: Expected harvest per hectare, used to calculate total nutrient demand.

businessContext_i18n.en: Expected harvest per hectare, used to calculate total nutrient demand.

inputs.label: Soil Nitrogen (N) Content
inputs.businessContext: Measured from soil test; influences the amount of N fertilizer needed.

businessContext_i18n.en: Measured from soil test; influences the amount of N fertilizer needed.

inputs.label: Soil Phosphorus (P) Content
inputs.businessContext: Measured from soil test; influences the amount of P fertilizer needed.

businessContext_i18n.en: Measured from soil test; influences the amount of P fertilizer needed.

inputs.label: Soil Potassium (K) Content
inputs.businessContext: Measured from soil test; influences the amount of K fertilizer needed.

businessContext_i18n.en: Measured from soil test; influences the amount of K fertilizer needed.

inputs.label: Application Efficiency Factor
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of applied fertilizer that is actually taken up by the crop; affected by method, timing, and weather.

businessContext_i18n.en: Percentage of applied fertilizer that is actually taken up by the crop; affected by method, timing, and weather.

inputs.label: Nitrogen Fertilizer Type
inputs.businessContext: Different sources have different N concentration and loss characteristics.

businessContext_i18n.en: Different sources have different N concentration and loss characteristics.

inputs.label: Phosphorus Fertilizer Type
inputs.businessContext: Different sources have different P concentration and solubility.

businessContext_i18n.en: Different sources have different P concentration and solubility.

rules.message: Soil nitrogen is high relative to target yield; consider reducing N application to avoid over-fertilization.
High rainfall forecast may reduce efficiency; consider split application or use of inhibitors.
Very dry soil; fertilizer may not dissolve properly. Consider irrigation or delayed application.

thresholds.message: Nitrogen application rate exceeds recommended limits for environmental safety.
Phosphorus application rate may cause runoff and eutrophication.

suggestedActions: Review inputs and verify results against site standards.
